2006.note: Post-college. Living with Jess's parents in West Virginia, working at Old Navy. Five hearts all year — music wasn't the priority.

2007.note: Back at Greenville as staff. Taste re-expanding — Weasel King discovery (wizard rock, 335 plays).

2008.note: 200 hearts — the highest year by far. Greenville staff era: Bloc Party, Vampire Weekend, Feist, Norah Jones alongside Ryan Adams. Everything was new. Everything mattered.

2009.note: 135 hearts. Still discovering aggressively — classic rock and alt-country dominating the heart button while indie rock dominated the scrobbles.

2010.note: Moved to Trinity International University. Laura Marling discovered — Lincoln Hall birthday show. 76 hearts, britpop rising in the loved column.

2011.note: Laura Marling at #1 album all-time. 102 hearts. The broadest genre spread of any year — 9 genres in the loved data.

2012.note: Moved to Austin. Founded Bravery Media. Hearts drop to 27. Building a business, not excavating music.

2013.note: 49 hearts. Emo starts appearing in the loved column for the first time — the return begins. Also jazz/soul hearts (Christopher Cross, yacht rock appreciation).

2014.note: Emo ties indie rock as top-loved genre. Into It. Over It. enters heavy rotation via Matt Herald. Mineral reunion shows begin.

2015.note: Britpop spikes in hearts (10) while barely registering in scrobbles. The heart button catches the Oasis/Stone Roses thread the volume doesn't.

2016.note: 18 hearts spread thin across 7 genres. Settled listening — the curation muscle resting.

2017.note: 5 hearts. The quietest year. Low period — Michelle Branch revisited.

2018.note: 11 hearts — 9 of them emo. When the heart button fires in a quiet year, it fires for the roots.

2020.note: 3 hearts. Pandemic. Two emo, one britpop.

2022.note: 20 hearts — 14 of them alt-country/folk. Echoing Green hits #1 for the year. Moved to Louisville. The synthwave precursor stirs.

2023.note: 51 hearts — 39 synthwave. The Midnight, FM-84, Timecop1983. A genuine new obsession, not just a phase. Hearts and scrobbles aligned for the first time in a decade.

2024.note: 22 hearts across 9 genres. Synthwave still leads but the spread returns — alt-country, classic rock, jazz all getting hearts.

2025.note: 14 hearts. Synthwave fading, indie rock and others rising. The transition begins.

2026.note: 17 hearts so far — 9 emo. The return to roots. Gates, Moving Mountains, Mineral. Building the visual identity demanded the sonic recentering.
